frameworks.name: high-dimensional neural population dynamics
frameworks.one_line: M1 activity occupies many population dimensions reflecting distinct control computations

frameworks.name: compositional / mixture-of-subskills control
frameworks.one_line: subskills are deployed flexibly, sometimes combined compositionally during skilled movement

frameworks.name: low-dimensional motor cortex view
frameworks.one_line: traditional assumption that M1 dynamics are low-dimensional and uniform

core_question: How does primary motor cortex flexibly combine multiple subskills, each with its own control strategy, to produce skilled movement?
key_assumptions: different behavioral control strategies map onto distinct neural subspaces
population factors can be selectively engaged or silenced by task demands
single external degree of freedom does not constrain neural dimensionality
manipulations: force-tracking task requiring different control strategies within a single DoF
epochs eliciting distinct subskills and their compositional combinations
key_findings: M1 activity is higher-dimensional than traditionally assumed
neural dynamics transition among many dimensions reflecting distinct computations
different control strategies occupy distinct neural locations and dimensions, sometimes used compositionally
groups of population factors activate only when their dynamics are needed, otherwise remain silent
interpretations: M1 hosts a flexible, high-dimensional repertoire of subskills rather than a single low-dimensional controller
engaged subskill dominates neural activity, so activity can differ even for matched motor output
